title: cyathea sylvatica
intro: Cyathea sylvatica, also known as the tree fern, is a species of evergreen fern native to the tropical rainforests of Australia and New Guinea. The plant's medicinal properties include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Cyathea sylvatica include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Cyathea sylvatica typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's fronds, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically to the affected area.
